questions.title: Use Environment Variables in a Vite React App
questions.task: Store a public API base URL in a Vite environment file, read it through import.meta.env, and throw a clear startup error when it is missing. Do not place secrets in client-side variables.
constraints: Use functional React components unless the task specifically requires a class component or error boundary.
Do not mutate props or state directly.
Use stable keys when rendering lists and clean up subscriptions, timers, or requests when required.
questions.hint: Only VITE_-prefixed variables are exposed to client code, and exposed values are public.
sourceQuestions: store credentials in ENV
use .env
use .env file
use .env in FE also
Use env for storing credentials or tokens
